pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 9 defines PATIENT_FNAME="Arthur"; patient created dynamically during task setup
setup_task.sh line 10 defines PATIENT_LNAME="Morgan"; patient created dynamically during task setup
verifier.py line 63 confirms DE is the standard code for Deceased in OSCAR EMR; verified against OSCAR EMR documentation that DE denotes deceased patient status

task_utils.sh line 140 uses login.jsp for Firefox startup; setup_oscar.sh line 295 sets Firefox homepage to login.jsp; docker-compose.yml line 44 also references login.do for healthcheck (both endpoints exist)
Default OSCAR EMR username confirmed by open-osp documentation; setup_oscar.sh line 217 configures this account

setup_oscar.sh lines 210-221 explicitly configure password to 'oscar' for this environment using SHA-1 hash. Note: Official OSCAR default is 'mac2002' per open-osp documentation, but this environment overrides it to 'oscar'
Standard OSCAR EMR second-level passcode confirmed by open-osp documentation; setup_oscar.sh line 217 confirms this PIN

privileged_info_summary: The task involves marking a synthetically created patient (Arthur Morgan) as deceased in OSCAR EMR. Arthur Morgan is a fictitious patient created during task setup (not from seed data). The target status code is DE (Deceased), which is OSCAR's standard code for deceased patients. Login credentials are username 'oscardoc' with password 'oscar' and PIN '1117'; note that while 'oscardoc' and PIN '1117' are OSCAR defaults, the password 'oscar' is environment-specific (official OSCAR default is 'mac2002', but this task environment overrides it). The OSCAR EMR instance runs on localhost:8080 at login endpoint /oscar/login.jsp. All metadata claims have been verified against the task setup scripts, verifier logic, and official OSCAR EMR documentation.
